status.next_allowed_action: Execute phase-3 task 3.1: define the graph event target classifier. Start by reading the progress file and running git log --oneline -15 plus npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine.

decision_log.reason: The work spans shared graph-engine modules, workbench integration, offline HTML verification, browser behavior, and multiple regression classes.

decision_log.decision: Proceed with a gated vertical slice instead of local patches or renderer rewrite
decision_log.reason: The user rejected MVP patching, while the design document also rules out Canvas/WebGL migration before profiling proves a renderer bottleneck.

decision_log.decision: Do not bring in earlier temporary bugfix commits from codex/fix-graph-community-wheel-zoom
decision_log.reason: This branch should stay clean and solve the regression class through the new shared coordinate/gesture/state path.
decision_log.source: git log and user branch cleanliness question

phases.goal: Record baseline failures, public API, and repeatable regression probes

acceptance.check: Known interaction regressions are recorded with trigger, current behavior, expected behavior, and target phase
evidence: Recorded five regressions in task 0.1 verification: community wash wheel, node wheel, drag jump under zoom/pan, node movement constrained by mixed clamp/input handling, and hover/edge preview drift.
Each regression includes current trigger/cause, expected target behavior, and target phase in the execution plan: phases 3, 4, and 5.

Task 0.2 recorded the pure test probe shapes that will cover those regressions in geometry.test.ts, gestures.test.ts, overlays tests, and render-model fixtures without adding premature failing assertions.

acceptance.check: Current branch is confirmed clean and does not contain old temporary bugfix commits
evidence: git log --oneline -15 exited 0 on codex/graph-interaction-architecture and showed no old temporary graph bugfix commits from codex/fix-graph-community-wheel-zoom.
Old bugfix branch checked separately at 3312e08027b0f35be0073bddd44443ee8ab53c21 with merge-base 1e1248025206925b3367372928fc1ebc113d3960.

acceptance.check: Public graph-engine API baseline and old bugfix comparison are recorded
evidence: Public graph-engine API baseline recorded for createGraphEngine, GraphEngineOptions, GraphEngineCapabilities, GraphEngine methods, StaticGraphRenderer methods, workbench host usage, and offline HTML usage.
Old bugfix branch comparison recorded as a renderer-level patch that must not be copied directly into this architecture branch.

acceptance.check: Workbench and offline HTML baseline behavior is recorded
evidence: Workbench baseline recorded from isolated http://127.0.0.1:5181/ against backend 8787 using AI学习知识库; evidence JSON /tmp/llm-wiki-graph-workbench-baseline.json, screenshot /tmp/llm-wiki-graph-workbench-baseline.png.

Workbench evidence includes community/global graph snapshot, node hover, node drag, node click/right drawer, and wheel behavior over blank/node/community wash with concrete transform and offset values.

Offline baseline recorded from /tmp/llm-wiki-offline-basic-l2YCnx/graph-interactive-basic/wiki/knowledge-graph.html; evidence JSON /tmp/llm-wiki-graph-offline-baseline.json, screenshot /tmp/llm-wiki-graph-offline-baseline.png.
Offline evidence includes wheel, drag, hover, localStorage pin persistence, and built-in reader behavior.

tasks.title: Record interaction regression, API, and old bugfix baseline
verification: git log --oneline -15 exited 0 on codex/graph-interaction-architecture; head was d88c2bb docs: add graph interaction execution plan and the visible top commits are design/plan records, not old temporary graph bugfix commits.
npm run test --workspace=@llm-wiki/graph-engine exited 0 before baseline recording: 41 suites, 150 tests, 150 pass, 0 fail.
Old branch codex/fix-graph-community-wheel-zoom exists at 3312e08027b0f35be0073bddd44443ee8ab53c21; merge-base with this branch is 1e1248025206925b3367372928fc1ebc113d3960.

Old branch recent commits include e1cf785 fix(graph): allow wheel zoom over community washes, daaa974 fix(graph): allow wheel zoom over nodes, and 80e9e14 fix(graph): keep dragged nodes under cursor; these fixes are not present in the current branch log.

git diff codex/graph-interaction-architecture..codex/fix-graph-community-wheel-zoom -- packages/graph-engine/src/render/static-renderer.ts packages/graph-engine/test/viewport.test.ts showed a renderer-level patch: 2 files changed, 112 insertions, 14 deletions; it added screenPointToRendererPoint, rendererPointDeltaToLayerDelta, node drag offset handling, and viewport tests. This plan must not copy that local patch directly because it keeps interaction math in the renderer instead of the planned Geometry/Gesture/Runtime State path.

Public API baseline recorded: packages/graph-engine/src/index.ts exports createGraphEngine(container, options); GraphEngine in packages/graph-engine/src/types.ts exposes applyDiff, isDragging, focusNode, focusCommunity, setTypeFilters, resetView, select, clearSelection, clearInteraction, setTheme, setPins, resetLayout, and destroy.

GraphEngineOptions baseline recorded at packages/graph-engine/src/types.ts: data, optional pins, theme, optional focus, optional typeFilters, optional toolbarContainer, and optional capabilities. GraphEngineCapabilities baseline recorded: persistPins, onAsk, onOpenPage, onSelectionChange, onSelectionClear, and onDragStateChange.

StaticGraphRenderer baseline recorded at packages/graph-engine/src/render/static-renderer.ts: render, applyDiff, isDragging, setTheme, setPins, focusNode, focusCommunity, setTypeFilters, resetView, select, clearSelection, clearInteraction, resetLayout, destroy.

Workbench host baseline recorded: workbench/web/src/components/GraphPanel.tsx calls createGraphEngine(hostRef.current, { data, pins, theme, capabilities: { onOpenPage, onSelectionChange, onSelectionClear, onAsk, persistPins, onDragStateChange } }).

Offline HTML baseline recorded: scripts/build-graph-html.sh checks window.LlmWikiGraphEngine.createGraphEngine and calls createGraphEngine(root, { data: graphData, pins, theme, toolbarContainer, capabilities: { persistPins } }).

Regression baseline recorded: wheel over community wash and wheel over node are blocked because the root wheel handler returns unless isBlankViewportTarget(event.target), and isBlankViewportTarget excludes .node and .community-wash.

Regression baseline recorded: drag under zoom/pan uses eventToGraphPoint(root, event), which converts root-relative client coordinates directly into WORLD_WIDTH/WORLD_HEIGHT and clamps to 0..1000 / 0..680, ignoring viewport pan/zoom and grab offset.

Regression baseline recorded: LiveGraphSimulation.dragTo also clamps drag target to 0..1000 / 0..680, so node movement cannot exceed the canonical world extent and constraints are currently mixed with drag input handling.

Regression baseline recorded: hover preview and edge preview use rootRect.width * node.x / 100 and rootRect.height * node.y / 100 instead of projected screen anchors, so they drift after viewport transforms or drawer resize.

Regression baseline recorded: community wash is a clickable SVG ellipse that stops click propagation; render/model.ts already has core-point wash logic that avoids chasing one far outlier, and later phases must preserve the visual-region-not-fence rule while adding bounded response evidence.

geometry.test.ts probe shape recorded for phase 1: worldPointToScreenPoint and screenPointToWorldPoint inverse checks under scale 0.5, 1, and 2.25; viewport pan/zoom projection; drawer/host resize anchor behavior; root client point to graph screen point; screen point to world point without silent drag-target clamping; explicit client/screen/world/layer/svg/minimap coordinate naming.

gestures.test.ts probe shape recorded for phase 3: wheel over blank, node, community wash, and edge returns zoom; wheel over search, toolbar, legend, drawer, minimap, and text editing control returns blocked; pointerdown over node returns node drag candidate; pointerdown over community wash returns community click candidate without blocking wheel; pointerdown over blank returns blank pan candidate.

gesture state-machine probe shape recorded for phase 3: click-vs-drag threshold, node drag start/move/end intents, no drawer click after drag, community click cancellation after movement threshold, pointercancel and lostpointercapture cleanup, and shared handling for mouse wheel and touchpad scroll.

overlay and wash probe shape recorded for phase 5 follow-through: projected node hover anchor, projected edge midpoint anchor, preview bounds after viewport/drawer resize, community wash non-fence behavior, and capped outlier wash response fixtures.

No test helper or fixture was created in task 0.2 because no passing helper is needed before Geometry/Gesture modules exist; this avoids committing empty scaffolding or failing regression assertions ahead of the implementation phase.

state.test.ts covers viewport, node hover, community hover, edge hover, single-node selection, multi-node selection, community focus, pins, positions, node-drag gesture, viewport-pan gesture, community-click gesture, and clearInteraction.

acceptance.check: Renderer reads runtime state snapshots without moving host drawer state into graph-engine
evidence: packages/graph-engine/src/render/static-renderer.ts now initializes createGraphRuntimeState with viewport, render positions, pins, selection, and focus.
Renderer render() syncs pins, selection, and focus into GraphRuntimeState, then rebuilds the renderable graph from the runtime snapshot rather than independent renderer-only state.
Viewport commit, wheel zoom, blank pan, resize anchoring, minimap viewport updates, and search focus centering read or write the runtime viewport snapshot.
Node hover, edge hover, and community hover targets are mirrored into runtime hover state, while hover positioning remains unchanged for the later overlay phase.
Pin set/reset, pin unpin, drag-end pin writes, and simulation cold-start fixed positions read or write the runtime pin snapshot.
Node drag and blank pan mark runtime active gesture state; isDragging now reads the runtime gesture snapshot.
Host drawer, search input text, toolbar panel state, and diff animation state remain outside GraphRuntimeState.

Created packages/graph-engine/src/render/state.ts with GraphRuntimeState, createGraphRuntimeState, explicit snapshot/update APIs, and types for hover, focus, active gesture, and listeners.
GraphRuntimeState owns viewport, committed positions, pin snapshots, hover target, selection/focus graph item, and active gesture without moving host drawer/search/toolbar/diff state into the module.
GraphRuntimeState clones inputs and snapshots so renderer or host callers cannot mutate hidden graph state by reference.
Exported GraphRuntimeState and related types through packages/graph-engine/src/render/index.ts.

Added packages/graph-engine/test/state.test.ts with 6 tests covering defaults, viewport/hover/selection/focus/pins/positions/gesture updates, subscriptions, simulation proposal commit boundaries, cloned snapshots, and clearInteraction behavior.

verification: Read progress file and plan; current unit was phase-2 task 2.2.

packages/graph-engine/src/render/static-renderer.ts now imports createGraphRuntimeState and initializes a graph-local runtime state owner with viewport, current positions, pins, selection, and focus.
render() now syncs incoming renderer state into GraphRuntimeState and rebuilds the renderable graph from the runtime snapshot for pins, selected node or selection, and community focus.
applyMotionFrame now commits simulation positions into GraphRuntimeState before rebuilding the graph from the runtime snapshot.
Viewport update paths for commitViewport, wheel zoom, blank pan, resize, minimap viewport, and search focus centering now read or write the runtime viewport snapshot.
Pin update paths for setPins, resetLayout, node double-click unpin, drag-end pin persistence, and simulation fixed-position startup now read or write the runtime pin snapshot.
Hover paths for node preview, edge preview, and community legend hover now mirror their active target into GraphRuntimeState while preserving the existing preview positioning behavior for phase 5.
Active node-drag and blank-pan gestures now write runtime active gesture state, and StaticGraphRenderer.isDragging reads that runtime gesture state.
Host drawer, search query/input state, toolbar panel state, and diff animation state were not moved into GraphRuntimeState.

Task 2.2 intentionally did not change user-visible gesture behavior: wheel target classification remains for phase 3, drag projection remains for phase 4, and projected overlay positioning remains for phase 5.
